summary: Soundboard recording of the June‑1991 Denver stadium show is generally clear and listenable, highlighted by a memorable Dark Star tease in Wharf Rat, strong Eyes of the World and an electric Picasso Moon.
review: The Miller SBD transfer captures the band’s late‑summer ’91 energy with good clarity – most reviewers praise its “impeccable” or “great” sound, though a few note occasional mix quirks. Musically the set is praised for the Dark Star tease embedded in Wharf Rat, an inspired Eyes of the World, a fiery Iko Iko and a surprisingly effective Picasso Moon; a handful of heads felt some moments (e.g., Crazy Fingers on Picasso) fell flat. Overall the performance is viewed as solid but not iconic.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the show 4‑5 stars for performance and sound, but a notable minority give 2‑3 stars citing weak spots and recording hiccups; weighting musical merit (70 %) over technical quality (30 %) yields a mid‑high rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Most comments describe the transfer as crisp, clear and “impeccable”; a few mention occasional mix balance issues (keyboard density, occasional drop‑outs) but nothing that makes it unlistenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set is described as solid with a few high‑energy moments; the second set shines thanks to an extended Eyes of the World, a compelling Picasso Moon/​Crazy Fingers jam and the memorable Dark Star tease inside Wharf Rat, giving the show a strong second‑half momentum.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers note his “look on Mickey’s face” while Jerry teases Dark Star; his guitar tone is praised as perfect in several posts.
band_member_comments.Phil: Comments range from Phil possibly kicking a speaker to him delivering a “frenetic bass pattern” that drives the second set and a solid Box encore.
band_member_comments.Bob: Keyboardists (Bruce/Floyd/Vince) are called “ham‑handed”, “dense” and at times fighting for bandwidth; still deemed good enough by most listeners.
band_member_comments.Keys: Both keyboard players receive criticism for being too thick/dense, but also credit for contributing to the jam dynamics on Picasso Moon and Crazy Fingers.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey’s facial expression is highlighted repeatedly; reviewers describe a “wave” of drum volume and overall powerful drumming throughout.
